title: Will Randy Feenstra win the Iowa GOP gubernatorial nomination (June 2)?

current.lean_note: I lean YES (my prob 80%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
current.rationale_summary: CORRECTED (was an over-reaction to 0.58): a single primary poll and unsanctioned county straw polls are too noisy to move much. Weighting the RELIABLE signals - Trump endorsement (May 29) and the real 35%-threshold->likely-convention mechanic - Feenstra remains a strong favorite (establishment + presidential backing carry a convention too); I hold ~0.80, only a modest discount below the market for genuine convention uncertainty.
key_drivers: likely convention but establishment+Trump favored there

history.rationale_summary: Feenstra got a late Trump endorsement (very strong in Iowa) but polls ~tied with Lahn (22 vs 24) with 27% undecided and a 35% threshold likely forcing a June 13 convention; the market's ~93% looks overconfident given that contested-convention path.
